prompt: {"agency":"skip","audience":"Systems integrators","budget":"$10k - $50k","channels":"linkedin,email","notes":"Focus on AU market","product":"Rubix Edge Controller","timeline":"1 quarter"}
result:
# Marketing Plan: Rubix Edge Controller — AU Market

**Target Audience:** Systems Integrators (Building Automation / IoT)
**Budget:** $10k–$50k | **Timeline:** Q3 2026 (1 Quarter) | **Primary Channels:** LinkedIn, Email

---

## 1. Key Messaging & Value Propositions

**Core Positioning:**
> Rubix Edge Controller — the open, protocol-agnostic edge platform that lets systems integrators deliver smarter buildings faster, with less vendor lock-in.

**Value Propositions for AU Systems Integrators:**

| Message | Why It Resonates |
|---|---|
| **Protocol-agnostic integration** — BACnet, Modbus, LoRa, REST APIs from a single device | SIs juggle multiple protocols across retrofit and greenfield jobs; one controller simplifies the stack |
| **Reduce project delivery time** — pre-built drivers and a visual configuration layer cut commissioning hours | Time-on-site is the #1 cost driver for AU integrators; faster commissioning = better margins |
| **Open platform, no lock-in** — Linux-based, API-first architecture lets you own the customer relationship | AU SIs are wary of being locked into a single OEM's ecosystem; openness is a competitive differentiator |
| **Local-first edge compute** — data processing and control logic runs on-prem, even when the cloud is unreachable | Critical for AU sites with unreliable connectivity (regional, industrial, remote campuses) |
| **Australian-supported** — NubeIO is AU-based with local engineering, support, and stock | De-risks procurement and support vs. importing from overseas vendors with timezone mismatches |

**Proof Points to Develop:**
- 2–3 AU case studies (e.g. commercial HVAC retrofit, multi-site energy monitoring)
- Quantified stat: "X% reduction in commissioning time" from a real deployment
- Partner testimonial from an existing AU SI

---

## 2. Channel Strategy

### LinkedIn (Primary — ~40% of budget)

| Tactic | Detail | Est. Cost |
|---|---|---|
| **Company page content** | 2 posts/week: technical tips, case study snippets, product updates, industry commentary (NABERS, Section J, HVAC trends) | Internal effort |
| **Sponsored content ads** | Target: AU-based job titles — "Systems Integrator", "BMS Engineer", "Controls Engineer", "Building Automation", "HVAC Controls" at companies with 10–200 employees | $4k–$8k/quarter |
| **LinkedIn Lead Gen Forms** | Gate a technical whitepaper or ROI calculator behind a lead form; lower friction than landing pages | Included in ad spend |
| **Founder/team thought leadership** | Personal posts from NubeIO leadership on open-source edge, AU building tech trends | Internal effort |

**Content Calendar Themes (rotate weekly):**
- Week 1: Technical how-to / integration tip
- Week 2: Customer story or use case
- Week 3: Industry trend / regulation commentary
- Week 4: Product feature spotlight

### Email (Primary — ~20% of budget)

| Tactic | Detail | Est. Cost |
|---|---|---|
| **Lead nurture sequence** | 5-email drip for new leads: (1) Welcome + positioning, (2) Technical overview PDF, (3) Case study, (4) ROI comparison, (5) Book a demo CTA | $1k–$2k (tooling + copywriting) |
| **Monthly newsletter** | Curated for SIs: product updates, integration guides, AU industry news | Internal effort |
| **Re-engagement campaign** | Target cold leads / past enquiries with a "what's new" angle | Internal effort |
| **Tooling** | Email platform (Mailchimp/Brevo free-tier may suffice at early volumes) | $0–$500/quarter |

### Content Assets (Supporting — ~30% of budget)

| Asset | Purpose | Est. Cost |
|---|---|---|
| **Technical whitepaper** — "The SI's Guide to Open Edge Controllers" | Lead magnet for LinkedIn + email | $2k–$4k (freelance writer + internal review) |
| **ROI calculator** (spreadsheet or simple web tool) | Lets SIs estimate commissioning time savings | $1k–$3k |
| **2 × customer case studies** (1-pager each) | Social proof for mid-funnel leads | $1k–$2k |
| **Product comparison sheet** — Rubix vs. Tridium/Honeywell/Schneider | Arms SIs with switching justification | Internal effort |
| **Short demo video** (2–3 min, screen capture + voiceover) | LinkedIn + email embed, landing page | $1k–$2k |

### Events (Supplementary — ~10% of budget)

| Tactic | Detail | Est. Cost |
|---|---|---|
| **1 × webinar** — "Open Edge for SIs: Live Demo + Q&A" | Lead gen + pipeline acceleration; promote via LinkedIn + email | $500–$1k (platform + promotion) |
| **ARBS 2026 / BuildingIQ meetup attendance** | If timing aligns — networking, not a booth. Meet SIs face-to-face | $1k–$2k (travel + collateral) |

---

## 3. Timeline & Milestones

### Month 1 — Foundation

- [ ] Finalise messaging framework and SI persona
- [ ] Set up LinkedIn ad account, audiences, tracking (UTMs, LinkedIn Insight Tag)
- [ ] Set up email platform + lead nurture sequence (5 emails)
- [ ] Commission whitepaper and begin case study interviews
- [ ] Launch LinkedIn organic posting cadence (2x/week)
- [ ] **Milestone:** First LinkedIn ad campaign live by end of Month 1

### Month 2 — Acceleration

- [ ] Publish whitepaper; launch as gated lead magnet on LinkedIn Lead Gen Forms
- [ ] Publish first case study; distribute via email + LinkedIn
- [ ] Launch monthly newsletter (first edition)
- [ ] Record and publish demo video
- [ ] Host webinar — promote 2 weeks in advance
- [ ] **Milestone:** 50+ marketing-qualified leads (MQLs) captured

### Month 3 — Optimisation & Pipeline

- [ ] Publish second case study + comparison sheet
- [ ] Optimise LinkedIn ads based on Month 1–2 data (pause underperformers, scale winners)
- [ ] Run email re-engagement campaign to cold leads
- [ ] Deliver ROI calculator asset
- [ ] Attend 1 industry event / meetup if available
- [ ] **Milestone:** 20+ demo requests or sales conversations from marketing-sourced leads

---

## 4. Success Metrics & KPIs

| Metric | Target (end of quarter) | How to Measure |
|---|---|---|
| **Marketing Qualified Leads (MQLs)** | 100–150 | CRM / email platform |
| **Demo requests / sales conversations** | 20–30 | CRM pipeline stage |
| **LinkedIn ad CTR** | >0.8% | LinkedIn Campaign Manager |
| **Cost per lead (CPL)** | <$50 | Ad spend ÷ leads |
| **Email open rate** | >30% | Email platform |
| **Email click-through rate** | >4% | Email platform |
| **Whitepaper downloads** | 50+ | Landing page / Lead Gen Forms |
| **Webinar registrations** | 40+ (25+ attendees) | Webinar platform |
| **Pipeline value generated** | $100k+ (attributed to marketing) | CRM |

---

## Budget Summary

| Category | Low Estimate | High Estimate |
|---|---|---|
| LinkedIn ads | $4,000 | $8,000 |
| Content creation (whitepaper, case studies, video) | $5,000 | $11,000 |
| Email tooling + copywriting | $1,000 | $2,500 |
| Webinar + events | $1,500 | $3,000 |
| **Total** | **$11,500** | **$24,500** |

This leaves headroom within the $50k cap for scaling LinkedIn ads if early results are strong, or for a second webinar / additional content assets in Month 3.

---

**Next steps:** If you'd like, I can drill deeper into any section — LinkedIn ad copy drafts, the email nurture sequence, or the whitepaper outline.

result:
I'll use the marketing plan tool to generate this for you.# Marketing Plan: NubeIO New Controller

**Budget:** < $10,000 | **Timeline:** 1 Month | **Primary Channel:** LinkedIn

---

## 1. Key Messaging & Value Propositions

**Core Message:**
> "The new NubeIO controller — smarter edge computing for modern buildings, at a fraction of the cost."

**Value Propositions:**

| Pillar | Message | Proof Point |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| **Cost Efficiency** | Reduce hardware costs without sacrificing capability | Competitive pricing vs. legacy BMS controllers |
| **Open Protocol** | Works with BACnet, Modbus, LoRa — no vendor lock-in | Protocol support list, integration case studies |
| **Edge Intelligence** | Process data at the edge, act in real-time | On-device logic, local decision-making without cloud dependency |
| **Easy Deployment** | Commission in minutes, not days | Web-based configuration, zero-touch provisioning |
| **Scalability** | Start with one controller, scale to an entire portfolio | Fleet management via Rubix platform |

**Buyer Personas:**

- **Facility Managers** — want reliability, simplicity, lower operating costs
- **System Integrators** — want open protocols, fast commissioning, margin
- **Building Owners / Property Managers** — want ROI, energy savings, tenant comfort

---

## 2. Channel Strategy (LinkedIn-Focused)

### LinkedIn (Primary — 60% of budget = ~$6,000)

| Tactic | Details | Budget |
|--------|---------|--------|
| **Sponsored Posts** (2x/week) | Product features, use cases, comparison graphics | $3,000 |
| **LinkedIn Articles** (2 long-form) | "Why Open Controllers Matter in 2026", "Edge vs. Cloud in Building Automation" | $500 (design/editing) |
| **Targeted InMail Campaign** | Direct outreach to facility managers & integrators in AU/US/UK | $1,500 |
| **Video Content** (2 short demos) | Unboxing + commissioning walkthrough, protocol integration demo | $1,000 |

**Targeting Criteria:**
- Job titles: Facility Manager, Building Engineer, Controls Engineer, System Integrator, Energy Manager
- Industries: Commercial Real Estate, Property Management, HVAC, Building Automation
- Company size: 50–5,000 employees
- Regions: Australia, US, UK, SE Asia

### Content (20% of budget = ~$2,000)

| Asset | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| **Product One-Pager (PDF)** | Downloadable spec sheet for lead capture |
| **Landing Page** | Dedicated page with CTA → request demo / get pricing |
| **Blog Post** | SEO-optimized "Introducing the New NubeIO Controller" |
| **Comparison Infographic** | New controller vs. legacy BMS controllers |

### Email (10% of budget = ~$500)

| Email | Timing | Audience |
|-------|--------|----------|
| **Announcement** | Week 1 | Existing contacts & newsletter list |
| **Feature Deep-Dive** | Week 2 | Engaged openers from email 1 |
| **Case Study / Social Proof** | Week 3 | Full list |
| **Limited-Time Offer / CTA** | Week 4 | Engaged leads |

### Events / Community (10% of budget = ~$500)

| Tactic | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **LinkedIn Live / Webinar** | 30-min live demo, Q&A with engineering team (Week 3) |
| **Partner Co-Promotion** | Cross-post with distributor / integrator partners |

---

## 3. Timeline & Milestones

| Week | Focus | Key Deliverables |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| **Week 1** (Apr 15–21) | **Prep & Soft Launch** | Landing page live, product one-pager done, announcement email sent, first 2 LinkedIn posts published |
| **Week 2** (Apr 22–28) | **Awareness Push** | LinkedIn ad campaigns live, InMail outreach begins, blog post published, comparison infographic shared |
| **Week 3** (Apr 29–May 5) | **Engagement & Demo** | LinkedIn Live / webinar hosted, video demos published, feature deep-dive email sent, retargeting ads active |
| **Week 4** (May 6–12) | **Conversion & Wrap-Up** | CTA/offer email sent, lead follow-ups, campaign performance review, report & next-steps doc delivered |

---

## 4. Success Metrics & KPIs

| Metric | Target | How to Measure |
|--------|--------|----------------|
| **LinkedIn Impressions** | 50,000+ | LinkedIn Campaign Manager |
| **LinkedIn Engagement Rate** | > 2.5% | Likes, comments, shares / impressions |
| **Landing Page Visits** | 500+ | Google Analytics / UTM tracking |
| **Leads Generated** (form fills / demo requests) | 30+ | CRM / landing page form |
| **Email Open Rate** | > 25% | Email platform (Mailchimp, HubSpot, etc.) |
| **Email Click-Through Rate** | > 4% | Email platform |
| **Webinar Registrations** | 50+ | Registration form |
| **Webinar Attendees** | 25+ | Live attendance count |
| **Sales Conversations Started** | 10+ | CRM / sales team tracking |
| **Cost Per Lead** | < $200 | Total spend / leads generated |

---

## Budget Summary

| Category | Allocation |
|----------|-----------|
| LinkedIn Ads & InMail | $6,000 |
| Content Creation (design, copy, video) | $2,000 |
| Email Marketing | $500 |
| Webinar / Events | $500 |
| **Total** | **$9,000** |
| **Contingency** | **$1,000** |

---

## Next Steps

1. **This week:** Finalize product messaging and create landing page
2. **Assign owners:** Who handles content creation, ad management, email sends?
3. **Set up tracking:** UTM parameters, LinkedIn Insight Tag, CRM pipeline stage for "New Controller" leads
4. **Brief sales team:** Ensure they know the messaging and have the one-pager ready for follow-ups

Want me to dive deeper into any section, generate specific LinkedIn post copy, or create the landing page content?

result:
I'll use the marketing plan tool to generate this. Let me first load the tool schema.Let me research Delta Controls' marketing approach to inform the plan.No worries — I'll build the plan drawing on known Delta Controls marketing patterns (they're a well-established reference in B2B building automation). Here's your plan:

---

# Marketing Plan: NubeIO New Controller Launch

**Budget:** < $10,000 | **Timeline:** 1 month | **Focus Channels:** LinkedIn, Email, Paid

---

## 1. Key Messaging & Value Propositions

**Positioning (inspired by Delta Controls' approach):**
Delta Controls succeeds by emphasizing reliability, open protocols (BACnet), energy efficiency, and seamless integration. NubeIO should position against this with a **disruptor narrative**:

| Delta Controls Messaging | NubeIO Counter-Positioning |
|---|---|
| Enterprise-grade BAS | Enterprise capability, edge-native simplicity |
| Proprietary ecosystem lock-in | Open-source, protocol-agnostic (BACnet, Modbus, LoRa) |
| Legacy hardware refresh cycles | Modern, software-defined controller |
| Complex commissioning | Deploy in minutes, not days |

**Core Message:**
> "The open, intelligent controller for modern buildings — deploy faster, integrate anything, pay less."

**Three Value Pillars:**
1. **Open by design** — No vendor lock-in. Works with BACnet, Modbus, REST, MQTT, LoRa out of the box.
2. **Edge intelligence** — Run logic locally, manage remotely. Built-in support for AI-driven optimization.
3. **Lower TCO** — Fraction of the cost of legacy controllers with faster commissioning and fewer truck rolls.

**Proof points to develop:**
- Side-by-side cost comparison vs. Delta Controls / Tridium / Honeywell
- Commissioning time benchmark (minutes vs. hours)
- Integration count (protocols supported out of the box)

---

## 2. Channel Strategy

### LinkedIn (Primary — ~40% of budget = $4,000)

| Tactic | Details | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| **Sponsored Posts** | 3 posts/week for 4 weeks. Mix of: product demo clips (30-60s), customer pain-point posts, comparison infographics | $2,500 |
| **LinkedIn Ads (Sponsored Content)** | Target: Facility Managers, BAS Engineers, MEP Consultants, Energy Managers. Geo: AU, US, UK. Company size: 50-5000 | $1,500 |
| **Organic Content** | Founder/team posts, behind-the-scenes, "why we built this" narrative | $0 |

**Content types (follow Delta's playbook but modernized):**
- Short product demo videos (screen recordings, bench demos)
- "Did you know?" carousel posts comparing legacy vs. NubeIO approach
- Customer/partner testimonial quotes
- Technical deep-dives (protocol support, edge compute capabilities)

### Email (Secondary — ~25% of budget = $2,500)

| Tactic | Details | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| **Launch Sequence (4 emails)** | Week 1: Teaser/problem statement. Week 2: Product reveal + key specs. Week 3: Technical deep-dive / use case. Week 4: Offer (early-bird pricing, free pilot) | $500 (tooling) |
| **List building** | LinkedIn Lead Gen Forms → email list. Website landing page with spec sheet download gate | $1,000 (landing page) |
| **Partner outreach** | Direct emails to system integrators, distributors, MEP firms | $1,000 (copywriting/design) |

**Segmented lists:**
- End users (facility managers, building owners)
- System integrators / VARs
- Consultants / specifiers
- Existing NubeIO customers

### Paid Search/Display (Tertiary — ~20% of budget = $2,000)

| Tactic | Details | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| **Google Ads** | Target keywords: "BACnet controller", "open building controller", "Delta Controls alternative", "IoT building automation controller" | $1,500 |
| **Retargeting** | Retarget landing page visitors across Google Display Network | $500 |

### Reserve (~15% = $1,500)

- Design assets (product photography, spec sheet layout, social templates)
- Contingency / amplify what's working mid-campaign

---

## 3. Timeline & Milestones

### Pre-Launch: Week 0 (Before Day 1)
- [ ] Finalize product spec sheet and photography
- [ ] Build landing page with lead capture form
- [ ] Set up email sequences in CRM/ESP
- [ ] Create LinkedIn ad creatives (3 variants for A/B testing)
- [ ] Draft all 4 email campaign messages
- [ ] Set up Google Ads campaigns
- [ ] Prep 12 LinkedIn organic posts (3/week × 4 weeks)

### Week 1: Awareness
- [ ] **Launch LinkedIn Ads** — awareness objective
- [ ] **Email #1** — "The problem with legacy controllers" (teaser)
- [ ] 3× LinkedIn organic posts (problem/pain-point focused)
- [ ] Google Ads go live
- **Milestone:** 5,000 LinkedIn impressions, 50 landing page visits

### Week 2: Reveal
- [ ] **Email #2** — Product reveal with specs + hero image
- [ ] 3× LinkedIn organic posts (product reveal, demo video, specs carousel)
- [ ] **Partner outreach emails** to top 20 integrators
- **Milestone:** 100 spec sheet downloads, 200 landing page visits cumulative

### Week 3: Educate
- [ ] **Email #3** — Technical deep-dive / use case spotlight
- [ ] 3× LinkedIn posts (comparison infographic, use case, technical post)
- [ ] Retargeting campaign activated for landing page visitors
- **Milestone:** 10 demo/pilot requests, 300 landing page visits cumulative

### Week 4: Convert
- [ ] **Email #4** — Early-bird offer / free pilot program CTA
- [ ] 3× LinkedIn posts (testimonial/social proof, urgency, recap)
- [ ] Direct follow-up calls/emails to warm leads
- [ ] Campaign performance review & optimization
- **Milestone:** 20 qualified leads, 5 pilot commitments

---

## 4. Success Metrics & KPIs

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|---|---|---|
| **LinkedIn Impressions** | 50,000+ | LinkedIn Campaign Manager |
| **LinkedIn Engagement Rate** | > 2.5% | LinkedIn Campaign Manager |
| **Landing Page Visits** | 500+ | Google Analytics |
| **Spec Sheet Downloads** | 150+ | CRM / form submissions |
| **Email Open Rate** | > 25% | ESP dashboard |
| **Email Click-Through Rate** | > 4% | ESP dashboard |
| **Qualified Leads (MQLs)** | 20+ | CRM pipeline |
| **Demo/Pilot Requests** | 10+ | CRM pipeline |
| **Cost Per Lead (CPL)** | < $500 | Budget / leads |
| **Pilot Commitments** | 5 | Sales team tracking |

### Weekly Reporting Cadence
- **Every Monday:** Review prior week's metrics, adjust ad spend toward best-performing creatives
- **Mid-campaign (end of Week 2):** Reallocate budget — double down on highest-ROI channel
- **End of Week 4:** Full campaign retrospective, document learnings for next launch

---

## Key Takeaway from Delta Controls' Approach

Delta Controls wins on **trust, reliability, and ecosystem depth** — built over decades. NubeIO can't out-legacy them. Instead, win on:

1. **Speed** — faster to deploy, faster to integrate, faster to value
2. **Openness** — no lock-in, works with everything
3. **Cost** — dramatically lower price point with modern architecture
4. **Story** — the "David vs. Goliath" narrative resonates on LinkedIn

The $10k budget is tight but workable for a focused 1-month sprint. The key is disciplined execution on LinkedIn + email with clear CTAs driving to one landing page.

prompt: what can you do?
result:
Here's what I can help you with:

## General Software Engineering
- **Read, write, and edit code** across your codebase
- **Debug issues** — trace bugs, read errors, and fix them
- **Refactor code** — restructure, rename, simplify
- **Explain code** — walk through logic, architecture, patterns
- **Run commands** — build, test, lint, deploy via the terminal
- **Git operations** — commits, branches, diffs, PRs
- **Search** — find files, grep for symbols, explore the codebase

## Your Project-Specific MCP Tools
You have a `nube` MCP server with tools for:

- **Rubix Developer** — manage devices (create, list, get, update, restart), read device points, write points, check health, get summaries
- **Nube Marketing** — generate marketing plans, content reviews (including QA variants)
- **Nube Admin** — create skill QA
- **Info** — get system info

## Other Capabilities
- **Web search & fetch** — look things up online
- **PR reviews** — review pull requests for quality/security
- **Security reviews** — audit pending changes for vulnerabilities
- **Scheduling** — set up recurring tasks or cron-based agents
- **Google integrations** — Gmail, Calendar, Drive (via authentication)
- **Jupyter notebooks** — read and edit `.ipynb` files
- **Plan & architect** — design implementation strategies before coding

## Skills (slash commands)
`/commit`, `/review`, `/security-review`, `/init`, `/simplify`, `/claude-api`, and more.

What would you like to work on?

prompt: {"agency":"skip","audience":"Systems integrators","budget":"$10k - $50k","channels":"linkedin,email","notes":"Focus on AU market","product":"Rubix Edge Controller","timeline":"1 quarter"}
result:
# Marketing Plan: Rubix Edge Controller — AU Market

**Target Audience:** Systems Integrators (Building Automation / IoT)
**Budget:** $10k – $50k | **Timeline:** Q3 2026 (1 quarter) | **Primary Channels:** LinkedIn, Email

---

## 1. Key Messaging & Value Propositions

### Core Positioning
> *Rubix Edge Controller: The open, programmable edge platform that lets systems integrators deliver smarter buildings — faster.*

### Value Propositions (ranked by SI priority)

| # | Message | Why It Resonates with SIs |
|---|---------|--------------------------|
| 1 | **Protocol-agnostic integration** — BACnet, Modbus, LoRa, and more from a single controller | SIs juggle multi-vendor sites; one box reduces truck rolls and spares inventory |
| 2 | **Open & programmable** — no vendor lock-in, extensible with custom logic and apps | SIs protect margins by owning the software layer, not renting it |
| 3 | **Remote-first management** — deploy, configure, and troubleshoot from anywhere via Rubix Cloud | AU geography means sites are spread across vast distances; remote access cuts travel costs |
| 4 | **Lower project cost** — competitive hardware + no per-point licensing | Helps SIs win tenders on price without sacrificing capability |
| 5 | **AU-based support & stock** — local engineering team, local warehouse | De-risks supply chain vs. importing from overseas with long lead times |

### Proof Points to Develop
- 1–2 AU case studies (target: commercial HVAC retrofit, multi-site retail/facility)
- TCO comparison vs. incumbent controllers (Tridium/Niagara, Schneider, Honeywell)
- Live demo or sandbox environment SIs can access themselves

---

## 2. Channel Strategy

### LinkedIn (Primary — ~40% of effort)

| Tactic | Detail | Cadence |
|--------|--------|---------|
| **Company page content** | Technical posts, mini case studies, product tips, industry commentary | 3×/week |
| **Sponsored content ads** | Targeted at AU-based SIs, BMS engineers, facility managers | Ongoing, A/B tested fortnightly |
| **LinkedIn Lead Gen Forms** | Gated asset (e.g. "SI Guide to Open Edge Controllers") driving email capture | 1 campaign/month |
| **Personal thought leadership** | Nube founders/engineers posting insights, tagging AU SI community | 2×/week |
| **LinkedIn Groups & comments** | Engage in ASHRAE AU, BACnet Interest Group, Smart Buildings AU groups | Daily |

**Targeting criteria:** Australia, job titles containing *systems integrator, BMS engineer, controls engineer, building automation, HVAC controls, project engineer*; companies in mechanical/electrical contracting, BMS, and energy services.

**Budget allocation:** $3k–$8k/month on LinkedIn Ads (sponsored content + lead gen forms)

### Email (Primary — ~35% of effort)

| Tactic | Detail | Cadence |
|--------|--------|---------|
| **Nurture sequence** | 5-email drip for new leads: intro → pain points → case study → demo invite → offer | Triggered on signup |
| **Monthly newsletter** | Product updates, AU project highlights, technical tips, upcoming events | Monthly |
| **Targeted outreach** | Personalised 1:1 emails to top 50 AU SI firms (research-backed, not spam) | Weeks 1–4, then follow-ups |
| **Event follow-up** | Post-webinar/post-event sequences | As needed |

**Tooling:** Use an email platform (e.g. Mailchimp, HubSpot Starter) with CRM integration. Budget: $500–$1k/quarter for tooling.

**List building:**
- LinkedIn Lead Gen Form captures
- Website form (gated content downloads)
- Purchased/rented list of AU mechanical contractors and BMS firms (use cautiously, comply with AU Spam Act 2003)
- Manual research of top 100 AU SI firms from AMCA, AIRAH, and BACnet AU member directories

### Supporting Channels (~25% of effort)

| Channel | Tactic | Budget |
|---------|--------|--------|
| **Webinars** | 1 live technical webinar per month (e.g. "Migrating Legacy BMS to Open Edge") | $500/webinar (promotion) |
| **Content/SEO** | 2 blog posts/month on nube-io.com targeting AU SI search terms | Internal effort |
| **Industry events** | Attend 1–2 AU trade events (ARBS, Total Facilities, AIRAH events) as visitor or small exhibitor | $2k–$5k if exhibiting |
| **Partner co-marketing** | Joint content with AU distributors or complementary vendors (sensors, cloud platforms) | In-kind |

---

## 3. Timeline & Milestones (Q3 2026)

### Month 1 — Foundation (Weeks 1–4)

- [ ] Finalise messaging framework and 1-page SI sales sheet (PDF)
- [ ] Publish gated asset: *"The SI's Guide to Open Edge Controllers"*
- [ ] Set up LinkedIn ad campaigns (2 audiences, 2 creatives each)
- [ ] Build email nurture sequence (5 emails)
- [ ] Research and compile target list of top 100 AU SI firms
- [ ] Launch personalised outreach to first 25 firms
- [ ] Publish 2 blog posts + 6 LinkedIn organic posts
- **Milestone:** Ad campaigns live, nurture sequence active, first leads captured

### Month 2 — Acceleration (Weeks 5–8)

- [ ] Host first live webinar (*"Open Edge for Multi-Site BMS"*)
- [ ] Launch second LinkedIn Lead Gen campaign with new gated asset (TCO calculator or comparison guide)
- [ ] Outreach to next 25 SI firms
- [ ] Publish 1 AU case study (even if brief/anonymised)
- [ ] Send first monthly newsletter
- [ ] Optimise LinkedIn ads based on Month 1 data (pause underperformers, scale winners)
- **Milestone:** 50+ marketing-qualified leads (MQLs), first demo requests from inbound

### Month 3 — Conversion & Review (Weeks 9–13)

- [ ] Host second webinar (*"Live Demo: Rubix Edge in a Retrofit Project"*)
- [ ] Complete outreach to remaining 50 SI firms
- [ ] Retarget engaged-but-not-converted leads via LinkedIn + email
- [ ] Attend 1 industry event (if timing aligns) or host a small in-person SI roundtable in Sydney/Melbourne
- [ ] Publish second case study
- [ ] Quarter-end performance review and planning for Q4
- **Milestone:** Pipeline value identified, first SI partner agreements or POC commitments

---

## 4. Success Metrics & KPIs

| Category | KPI | Q3 Target |
|----------|-----|-----------|
| **Awareness** | LinkedIn impressions | 100,000+ |
| **Awareness** | Website visits from AU | 1,500+ unique visitors |
| **Lead Gen** | Marketing-qualified leads (MQLs) | 75–150 |
| **Lead Gen** | Email list size (AU SIs) | 300+ |
| **Engagement** | LinkedIn engagement rate | >2% |
| **Engagement** | Email open rate | >30% |
| **Engagement** | Email click-through rate | >5% |
| **Engagement** | Webinar attendees | 25+ per session |
| **Pipeline** | Demo requests / POC conversations | 15–25 |
| **Pipeline** | Qualified sales opportunities | 5–10 |
| **Revenue** | SI partner agreements or first orders | 2–3 |

### Tracking & Reporting
- **Weekly:** LinkedIn ad performance, email campaign metrics
- **Fortnightly:** Lead quality review with sales (MQL → SQL conversion)
- **Monthly:** Full dashboard review, budget reconciliation, content calendar update
- **End of quarter:** ROI analysis, cost-per-lead, pipeline attribution report

---

## Budget Breakdown (Indicative)

| Line Item | Low ($10k) | Mid ($30k) | High ($50k) |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| LinkedIn Ads | $5,000 | $15,000 | $24,000 |
| Email tooling | $500 | $1,000 | $1,500 |
| Content creation (design, copywriting) | $2,000 | $5,000 | $8,000 |
| Webinar platform + promotion | $1,000 | $2,000 | $3,000 |
| Event attendance/exhibiting | $0 | $4,000 | $8,000 |
| Target list / data enrichment | $500 | $1,000 | $2,000 |
| Contingency | $1,000 | $2,000 | $3,500 |
| **Total** | **$10,000** | **$30,000** | **$50,000** |

---

## Key Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| Low brand awareness in AU SI market | Lead with technical credibility — engineering-led content, not marketing fluff |
| SIs are conservative / loyal to incumbents | Focus on *complement, then replace* messaging; offer risk-free POC |
| Small addressable audience on LinkedIn | Tight targeting + frequency capping to avoid ad fatigue; rotate creatives fortnightly |
| Spam Act compliance (email) | Only email opted-in contacts or use legitimate business exception; include unsubscribe in every email |

---

This plan is designed to be executable by a small team (1–2 people + occasional contractor support for design/copy). The LinkedIn + email focus keeps things lean while directly reaching the people who specify and install controllers on AU job sites.

prompt: {"audience":"new controller","budget":"\u003c $10k","channels":"linkedin,email","notes":"na","product":"new controller","timeline":"1 month"}
result:
I'll use the Nube marketing plan tool to generate this for you.Here's a practical, actionable marketing plan for the **New Controller** launch:

---

# Marketing Plan: NubeIO New Controller

**Budget:** < $10k | **Timeline:** 1 month | **Primary Channels:** LinkedIn, Email

---

## 1. Key Messaging & Value Propositions

**Core Message:**
> "Smarter building control, simplified. The new NubeIO controller delivers edge intelligence for modern building automation — without the complexity or cost of legacy systems."

**Value Propositions:**

| Pillar | Message | Proof Point |
|---|---|---|
| **Simplicity** | Deploy in minutes, not days | Plug-and-play setup, web-based configuration |
| **Edge Intelligence** | Process data locally, act instantly | On-device logic, reduced cloud dependency |
| **Open & Interoperable** | Works with your existing systems | BACnet, Modbus, MQTT, REST API support |
| **Cost Efficiency** | Enterprise capability at SMB pricing | Lower hardware cost + reduced integration labor |
| **Scalability** | Start with one, scale to thousands | Fleet management via Rubix platform |

**Positioning Statement:**
For facility managers, system integrators, and building automation engineers who need reliable, cost-effective edge control — the NubeIO controller is the open, programmable alternative to locked-down legacy hardware.

---

## 2. Channel Strategy

### LinkedIn (Primary — ~40% of effort)

| Tactic | Details | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| **Organic posts** | 3x/week: product teasers, use cases, behind-the-scenes, customer pain points | $0 |
| **LinkedIn Ads** | Sponsored posts targeting job titles: BMS Engineer, Facility Manager, Controls Engineer, IoT Product Manager | $2,500 |
| **LinkedIn Articles** | 2 long-form pieces: "Why Edge Controllers Matter in 2026" + product deep-dive | $0 |
| **Employee advocacy** | Team members share and comment on posts | $0 |

**Targeting:** Industries: commercial real estate, HVAC, smart buildings, energy management. Geo: AU, US, UK. Company size: 50–5,000.

### Email (Primary — ~30% of effort)

| Sequence | Timing | Content |
|---|---|---|
| **Teaser** | Week 1 | "Something new is coming" — problem-focused, no product reveal |
| **Launch announcement** | Week 2 | Product reveal, key specs, hero image, CTA to landing page |
| **Technical deep-dive** | Week 3 | Architecture, protocols, integration guide, CTA to demo |
| **Social proof / Case study** | Week 4 | Early adopter results or use-case scenario, CTA to order |

**List segments:** Existing customers, trial users, integrator partners, inbound leads.
**Budget:** Email platform costs ~$0 (existing tooling assumed), copywriting/design ~$1,000.

### Content (Supporting — ~20% of effort)

| Asset | Purpose | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| **Landing page** | Central hub — specs, benefits, demo CTA | $500 (design) |
| **Product one-pager (PDF)** | Sales enablement, email attachment | $300 |
| **2-min product video** | LinkedIn, landing page, email embed | $2,000 |
| **Blog post** | SEO + organic traffic: "Introducing the New Controller" | $0 |
| **Comparison table** | vs. legacy controllers (Tridium, Distech, etc.) | $0 |

### Events (Lightweight — ~10% of effort)

| Tactic | Details | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| **Live LinkedIn webinar** | 30-min demo + Q&A, Week 3 | $0 |
| **Partner demo sessions** | 1:1 virtual demos for top integrator partners | $0 |

---

## 3. Timeline & Milestones

```
WEEK 1 — PRE-LAUNCH
├── Finalize messaging, landing page, one-pager
├── Send teaser email to segmented lists
├── Begin LinkedIn teaser posts (3x)
└── Product video in production

WEEK 2 — LAUNCH
├── Publish landing page + blog post
├── Send launch announcement email
├── LinkedIn launch post (boosted) + ad campaign starts
├── Share product video
└── Announce webinar date

WEEK 3 — EDUCATE
├── Send technical deep-dive email
├── Host live webinar / demo
├── LinkedIn: post webinar clips, technical content
├── Begin partner demo outreach
└── Publish comparison table

WEEK 4 — CONVERT
├── Send social proof / case study email
├── LinkedIn retargeting ads to landing page visitors
├── Sales follow-up on webinar attendees + email clickers
├── Collect feedback, iterate messaging
└── Report on KPIs
```

---

## 4. Success Metrics & KPIs

| Metric | Target | How to Measure |
|---|---|---|
| **Landing page visits** | 500+ unique visits | Analytics |
| **Email open rate** | > 30% | Email platform |
| **Email click-through rate** | > 5% | Email platform |
| **LinkedIn post impressions** | 10,000+ total | LinkedIn analytics |
| **LinkedIn ad CTR** | > 0.8% | LinkedIn Campaign Manager |
| **Webinar registrations** | 50+ | Registration form |
| **Webinar attendees** | 25+ | Webinar platform |
| **Demo requests** | 15+ | Landing page form |
| **Sales-qualified leads** | 10+ | CRM |
| **Units ordered (or pipeline created)** | 5+ orders / $50k pipeline | CRM |

---

## Budget Summary

| Item | Cost |
|---|---|
| LinkedIn Ads | $2,500 |
| Product Video | $2,000 |
| Email Copy & Design | $1,000 |
| Landing Page Design | $500 |
| Product One-Pager | $300 |
| Contingency / Tools | $700 |
| **Total** | **$7,000** |

---

## Quick-Start Checklist

- [ ] Lock in product specs and hero image from engineering
- [ ] Draft landing page copy and wireframe
- [ ] Segment email lists (customers, partners, leads)
- [ ] Set up LinkedIn Campaign Manager and audience targeting
- [ ] Brief video production (even a polished screen recording works at this budget)
- [ ] Write email sequence (4 emails)
- [ ] Schedule LinkedIn content calendar (12 posts over 4 weeks)
- [ ] Set up tracking: UTM parameters, landing page analytics, CRM tagging

---

This plan keeps spend under $7k, focuses effort on the two highest-ROI channels for B2B IoT (LinkedIn + email), and drives toward measurable pipeline within 30 days. Want me to drill deeper into any section — ad copy, email drafts, or the landing page structure?

prompt:
You are an AI app builder for NubeIO. Generate a tool for a shopping list app.

When generating a tool, output a JSON code block with this exact format:
```json:tool
{
  "name": "tool_name",
  "description": "...",
  "toolClass": "read-only",
  "params": {},
  "script": "function handle(params) { ... }"
}
```

The JS runtime has: config.get(key), log.info(msg).

Create a "check_off_item" tool that marks a shopping list item as checked/bought. It should take an item name param and toggle its checked status. Keep the script short.
result:
```json:tool
{
  "name": "check_off_item",
  "description": "Toggle the checked/bought status of a shopping list item",
  "toolClass": "read-write",
  "params": {
    "item_name": {
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Name of the shopping list item to check off or uncheck",
      "required": true
    }
  },
  "script": "function handle(params) { var list = JSON.parse(config.get('shopping_list') || '[]'); var found = false; for (var i = 0; i < list.length; i++) { if (list[i].name.toLowerCase() === params.item_name.toLowerCase()) { list[i].checked = !list[i].checked; log.info((list[i].checked ? 'Checked' : 'Unchecked') + ': ' + list[i].name); found = true; break; } } if (!found) { log.info('Item not found: ' + params.item_name); return { success: false, error: 'Item not found' }; } config.set('shopping_list', JSON.stringify(list)); return { success: true, item: list.find(function(i) { return i.name.toLowerCase() === params.item_name.toLowerCase(); }) }; }"
}
```

**What it does:**

- Takes an `item_name` parameter (string, required)
- Reads the shopping list from `config.get('shopping_list')`
- Finds the item by case-insensitive name match
- Toggles its `checked` boolean (`true` → `false`, `false` → `true`)
- Persists the updated list back via `config.set`
- Returns the updated item on success, or an error if the item wasn't found
- Set to `read-write` since it modifies state

You are an AI app builder for NubeIO. The user is building a store app and needs help creating tools and prompts.

When generating a tool, output a JSON code block with this exact format:
```json:tool
{
  "name": "tool_name",
  "description": "What the tool does",
  "toolClass": "read-only",
  "params": {
    "param_name": { "type": "string", "required": true, "description": "Param description" }
  },
  "script": "function handle(params) {\n  // Tool logic here\n  const result = http.get('https://example.com/api');\n  return { data: result };\n}"
}
```

When generating a prompt, output a JSON code block with this format:
```json:prompt
{
  "name": "prompt_name",
  "description": "What the prompt does",
  "body": "Markdown template body with {{variable}} placeholders"
}
```

The JS runtime has these APIs available:
- http.get(url), http.post(url, body), http.put(url, body), http.patch(url, body), http.delete(url)
- secrets.get(key) — get a secret from the user's app install settings
- config.get(key) — get a non-secret setting
- log.info(msg), log.warn(msg), log.error(msg)
- files.read(relativePath) — read a file relative to the app directory

HTTP calls are restricted to the app's allowedHosts list. The handle(params) function receives the tool's declared params.

Keep tool scripts concise. Ask clarifying questions if the user's request is ambiguous. Always explain what the generated tool/prompt does.

The user's app context:
App name: "test", Description: ""

User request: Create a tool that checks if a URL is reachable
result:
Here's a tool that checks if a URL is reachable by making an HTTP GET request to it:

```json:tool
{
  "name": "check_url",
  "description": "Checks if a URL is reachable by sending an HTTP GET request and returning the status",
  "toolClass": "read-only",
  "params": {
    "url": { "type": "string", "required": true, "description": "The URL to check (e.g. https://example.com)" }
  },
  "script": "function handle(params) {\n  try {\n    const resp = http.get(params.url);\n    return {\n      reachable: true,\n      url: params.url,\n      status: resp.status\n    };\n  } catch (e) {\n    return {\n      reachable: false,\n      url: params.url,\n      error: e.message\n    };\n  }\n}"
}
```

**What it does:**
- Takes a `url` parameter and sends an HTTP GET request to it
- Returns `reachable: true` with the HTTP status code on success
- Returns `reachable: false` with the error message if the request fails (DNS failure, timeout, connection refused, etc.)

**Note:** The URL's host must be listed in your app's `allowedHosts` configuration, otherwise the HTTP call will be blocked by the runtime sandbox.

You are helping the user query nodes in Rubix using the Haystack filter language.

The user wants to find: all

## How to Query

Call `rubix.query_filter` with orgId, deviceId, and a `filter` string using Haystack syntax.

## Haystack Filter Syntax

**By type:**
- `type is "core.trigger"` — exact type match
- `type in ["core.trigger", "core.counter"]` — multiple types

**By name:**
- `name contains "Supply"` — substring match
- `name like "valve%"` — wildcard

**By identity tags (fast):**
- `i has "sensor"` — has tag
- `i has ["temp", "humid"]` — has any of these tags
- `i hasAll ["temp", "sensor", "point"]` — has all tags

**By refs:**
- `siteRef is "node_001"` — at a specific site
- `equipRef is "ahu_01"` — belongs to equipment
- `parentRef is "dev_123"` — direct children

**By port values:**
- `out.valueNum > 20` — output value above 20
- `state.value is "active"` — string port value

**By alarm state:**
- `i has "alarm" and state.value is "active"` — active alarms
- `severity.value is "Critical"` — critical alarms

**By settings (JSONB):**
- `settings.productCode contains "widget"` — settings field search

**Combining:**
- `type is "sensor" and name contains "Temp"` — AND
- `i has "temp" or i has "humid"` — OR
- `not (status is "inactive")` — NOT
- `(i has "temp" or i has "humid") and siteRef is "site_1"` — grouping

**Modifiers (pipe):**
- `type is "device" | limit 50` — limit results
- `type is "device" | sort name asc` — sort
- `type is "device" | offset 10 | limit 10` — pagination
- `type is "trigger" | select out, in1` — port projection

## Instructions

1. Translate the user's natural language request into a Haystack filter
2. Call `rubix.query_filter` with the filter
3. Present the results in a clear table or list
4. If no results, suggest alternative filters
result: I need an `orgId` and `deviceId` to run the query. Do you have these, or should I look them up first?
